nomy, while a transition coordinator, if present, facilitates continuity and prevents loss of contact with medical teams (60).
It is strongly recommended that pediatric teams prepare comprehensive transfer documents summarizing the medical history, treatments received and future goals.
Joint clinic models, bringing together pediatricians and adult gastroenterologists, promote trust and information exchange (59,60). Available data remain limited, but show that struc-
tured programs improve medication adherence, reduce hospitalizations and increase patient satisfaction (59,60).
Treatment of pediatric Crohn’s disease
General principles of management
(Table 6)
The recommendations of ESPGHAN and ECCO (European Crohn’s and Colitis Organization) on the medical management of pediatric Crohn’s disease were updated in 2021 (62).
Therapeutic strategy must be individualized taking into account age, phenotype (Paris classification), location, disease behavior,
